cot_prefix: Okay, so I need to analyze this ethical dilemma about John. Let me start by understanding the situation. John is married, but he's falling in love with his co-worker. This puts him in a tough spot because if he pursues this new relationship, it could hurt his family, especially his wife and kids. But if he suppresses his feelings, he might feel unhappy or unfulfilled. First, I should consider the different ethical perspectives. Maybe deontological ethics, which is about rules and duties. John has a duty to his marriage, right? So he should stay loyal to his wife. Then there's utilitarianism, where the action should maximize overall happiness. If John leaves his family, maybe his co-worker and he would be happy, but it could cause a lot of pain to the wife and children. On the other hand, if John stays, maybe he's unhappy and that could also affect the family dynamics negatively. I also need to think about the rights of the individuals involved. John's right to personal happiness versus his family's right to not be harmed. Then there's the aspect of relationships and trust. If John acts on his feelings, it
